rules.rule_name: All Form Fields Initialised to Null
rules.description: On application startup, every ModelProperties entry (13 fields) is initialised with a null value inside PocModel. The presenter then syncs initial field states from the view on binding initialisation.
rules.action: Initialise all 13 ValueModel entries to null

rules.description: Every time the user types into or deletes from any text field, the corresponding model ValueModel is immediately updated with the complete current text content via DocumentListener.
rules.condition: User types in any text field (insertUpdate or removeUpdate)
rules.action: Update the bound ValueModel<String> with current document text

rules.description: When a gender radio button (Weiblich/Männlich/Divers) changes state, the corresponding Boolean ValueModel in the model is updated immediately via ChangeListener.
rules.action: Update the bound ValueModel<Boolean> with the isSelected() value

rules.description: The female (Weiblich) radio button is pre-selected by default when the form first renders. After a successful form submission, the gender is reset back to Female.
rules.condition: Application start OR successful form submission

rules.description: When the 'Anordnen' button is clicked, ALL 13 ModelProperties are collected into a flat HashMap<String,String> and submitted together as a JSON object in the HTTP POST body. No field is omitted, even if null.
rules.action: Serialise all 13 fields to JSON and POST to http://localhost:8080/post

rules.rule_name: Non-Empty HTTP Response Triggers Success Event
rules.description: After HTTP POST, if the response body is not empty, the full response body string is emitted as an event. This is the success path.

rules.rule_name: Empty HTTP Response Triggers Failure Event
rules.description: If the HTTP response body is empty (blank), a 'Failed operation' string is emitted instead of the response body.
When the EventEmitter fires (after any HTTP submission), the view is fully reset: all text fields are cleared, gender is reset to Female, and the server response is displayed in the text area.
rules.condition: EventEmitter fires with any event data
rules.action: Clear all text fields, reset gender to female, display response in textArea

rules.rule_name: Application Does Not Exit on Form Close
rules.description: The main thread is blocked by CountDownLatch(1) which is never decremented in the MVP application. The JFrame uses EXIT_ON_CLOSE so the window close still terminates the JVM directly.

rules.rule_name: HTTP POST Target is Fixed Localhost Endpoint
rules.description: The HTTP service target is hardcoded to http://localhost:8080/post and cannot be configured at runtime. This endpoint is expected to be the HTTPBin Docker container.
rules.action: POST JSON to http://localhost:8080/post with Content-Type: application/json

rules.description: In the WebSocket client, if an incoming JSON message has target='textarea', only the textArea component is updated with the message's 'content' value.

In the WebSocket client, if an incoming JSON message has target='textfield', the full JSON payload is parsed into a SearchResult and all form fields (name, first, dob, zip, ort, street, hausnr, iban, bic, valid_from) are populated.
rules.action: Parse JSON into SearchResult and populate all form text fields

rules.description: When the WebSocket connection is closed, the CountDownLatch is decremented to 0, unblocking the main thread and allowing the application to terminate.

The OpenAPI spec (api.yml) defines all 13 fields (FIRST_NAME, LAST_NAME, DATE_OF_BIRTH, STREET, BIC, ORT, ZIP, FEMALE, MALE, DIVERSE, IBAN, VALID_FROM, TEXT_AREA) as optional strings. No validation is enforced at the UI or model layer.
rules.action: All fields submitted regardless of value (no validation)

rules.rule_name: WebSocket Server Broadcasts to All Clients
rules.description: The Node.js WebSocket server (WebsocketServer.js) relays every message it receives to ALL connected clients without filtering or routing logic.
rules.condition: Any client sends a message to the WebSocket server
rules.action: Broadcast message to all connected clients including sender

rules.rule_name: Boolean Gender Values Stored as String in Submission
rules.description: In PocModel.action(), Boolean ValueModel values (MALE, FEMALE, DIVERSE) are serialised using .toString(), converting true/false to string literals 'true'/'false' in the POST payload.
rules.action: Boolean values are serialised as string 'true' or 'false' in JSON body

rules.description: PocPresenter wraps IOException and InterruptedException from model.action() in RuntimeException. This means checked exceptions from HTTP communication become unchecked and will crash the EDT if triggered.
rules.condition: HTTP POST fails or thread is interrupted during submission
rules.action: Throw RuntimeException — no user-visible error handling

rules.description: The WebSocket client expects incoming person data JSON to contain these keys: name, first, dob, zip, ort, street, hausnr, iban, bic, valid_from. Missing keys result in null values in SearchResult fields.
rules.action: Parse JSON fields; missing keys leave SearchResult fields as null

workflows.name: MVP Form Submission Workflow
workflows.description: End-to-end flow from user data entry to server response display in the MVP (PocPresenter/PocModel/PocView) application.

steps.action: Types data into any form field
Logs all field values to stdout
Checks response body; emits event (success or 'Failed operation')
Sets textArea to response, clears all fields, resets gender to Female
Reflects updated state to the user

workflows.name: WebSocket Data Receive Workflow
workflows.description: Flow for receiving and rendering server-pushed data in the WebSocket Swing client.

steps.action: Broadcasts JSON message to all connected clients
Populates all 10 text fields from SearchResult

workflows.name: Application Bootstrap Workflow
workflows.description: Startup sequence for the MVP Swing application.

steps.action: Subscribes to EventEmitter, wires button listener, calls initializeBindings()
